LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-09-2024 HEARING RE: PETITIONER DANIEL EVERETT'S MOTION FOR ADDITIONAL TIME TO FILE WRIT IN APPELLATE COURT (GC SECTION 7923.500(B)) AND REQUEST THAT RESPONDENT BE ORDERED TO FILE A PROPOSED ORDER CONCERNING THIS COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING OF MAY 1, 2024, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: COUNSEL FOR RESPONDENT IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER REGARDING THE MAY 2, 2024 COURT HEARING. THE COURT GRANTS PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL TIME TO FILE A WRIT IN THE APPELLATE COURT. PETITIONER SHALL HAVE 20 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE FORTHCOMING ORDER REGARDING THE MAY 2, 2024 COURT HEARING TO FILE A WRIT IN THE APPELLATE COURT. PETITIONER IS INSTRUCTED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT THE PROPOSED ORDER ON THE COURT'S GRANTING OF ADDITIONAL TIME. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Motion For Additional Time To File Writ In Appellate Court (Gc Section 7923.500(B)) And Request That Respondent Be Ordered To File A Proposed Order Concerning This Court'S Tentative Ruling Of May 1, 2024
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Petitioner DANIEL EVERETT (Tel: 415-426-8157) in pro per;
Atty. SUZANNE GRANDT, Esq. of the Office of General Counsel of the State Bar of California (Tel: 213-765-1248) for respondent the State Bar of California.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER DANIEL EVERETT's Motion For Additional Time To File Writ In Appellate Court (Gc Section 7923.500(B)) And Request That Respondent Be Ordered To File A Proposed Order Concerning This Court'S Tentative Ruling Of May 1, 2024.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Matter discussed.

The Court rules as follows:

Counsel for respondent is to prepare and submit the proposed order regarding the May 2, 2024 court hearing.

The Court grants Petitioner's request for additional time to file a writ in the Appellate Court. Petitioner shall have 20 days from notice of entry of the forthcoming order regarding the May 2, 2024 court hearing to file a writ in the Appellate Court. Petitioner is instructed to prepare and submit the proposed order on the Court's granting of additional time.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING ON AMENDED VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE DIRECTED TO THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA ORDERING COMPLIANCE WITH THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT
Off Calendar May-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Plaintiff DANIEL EVERETT, in pro per present (tel: 415-891-9472).
Atty. LISA JACOBS, Esq. of Office of the Attorney General Counsel of the State Bar of California (tel: 213-765-1248) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: AMENDED VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE DIRECTED TO THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA ORDERING COMPLIANCE WITH THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission. Counsel Lisa Jacobs is to provide the Court with exhibit 5 in a manila envelope indicating confidential content.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR 11975 (tel: 909-831-2644 / e-mail: csrjane@hotmail.com). (302/RBU)

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Petitioner DANIEL EVERETT, in pro per, present.
Atty. LISA JACOBS, Esq. of the Office of General Counsel - State Bar of California (tel:213-765-1248) for Respondent.

Hearing Re: STATUS CONFERENCE.

Matter discussed. Counsel Jacobs states the parties have met and conferred but no issues were resolved. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court orders as follows: Respondent may file an opposition to the writ no later than April 11, 2024. Petitioner may file a reply no later than April 18, 2024. The Court continues the matter to May 2, 2024 at 9:30 A.M. in Department 302. The Court states notice of the hearing scheduled to May 2, 2024 is waived.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
